event_name: Sundays in the Park: America's Big Band Music, Today and Yesterday
blurb:
The Roswell New Horizons Pops Band is a 40-piece ensemble, and this Sunday they are bringing out the Cool Blue Jazz Band plus a returning special guest act called the Stayin' Alive Crew for a full big band afternoon in the park. That is a lot of horn for a Sunday.

If you are spending Father's Day weekend looking for something that does not require a reservation or a parking garage, this is it. Bring a chair, bring a cooler if the venue allows it, and let a 40-person band play you into a Sunday afternoon the right way. Free, outside, and genuinely good music. Harder to find than it sounds.
scoring_notes: The empty nester segment of the Perimeter audience indexes well for live band and jazz events. Father's Day weekend timing gives it a natural hook. Free admission is a plus but not the main sell here. The 40-piece band and special guest acts give it more substance than a typical park performance. Slight skew toward older end of the audience demographic.

venue: Southern Post, 1055 Alpharetta Street, Roswell, GA
blurb:
Southern Post is throwing a block party to kick off summer, and they are timing it to run alongside Alive in Roswell so the whole street has energy. Live music, drinks, and the kind of crowd that actually shows up to enjoy a Thursday evening rather than grind through it.

This is a young professionals and social crowd kind of event. No agenda, no dress code, just a good outdoor Thursday night with people who live nearby. Roswell is a quick shot from the Perimeter, and if you have not made the drive to Southern Post yet, this is a solid reason to finally do it.
scoring_notes: Good fit for the young professional segment of the Perimeter audience. Free to attend keeps it low-friction. The pairing with Alive in Roswell adds energy and foot traffic. Slight geographic stretch from core Perimeter coverage area, but Southern Post is a known destination venue. Adult skew fits this newsletter's audience profile.

venue: Chattahoochee Nature Center, 9135 Willeo Rd, Roswell, GA
blurb:
48 years. That is how long this race has been running, literally, along the Chattahoochee River. The Possum Trot 10K is a Perimeter-area tradition, and the fact that it is capped at 800 participants means it still feels like a real community race rather than a chaos event with 10,000 strangers.

The course is flat and fast, which is not something you hear about many Atlanta-area races. It benefits programs at the Chattahoochee Nature Center, so you are doing something good while you do something good for yourself. Spots go. If you have been eyeing a summer race, register now and don't wait until the week of.
scoring_notes: Median age of 38 and mix of young professionals and young families maps well to active outdoor events. The Chattahoochee Nature Center is a known Perimeter-area institution. 800-person cap creates genuine scarcity. Annual event but tied to a specific date and registration deadline, so it has real urgency.

venue: Springs Cinema & Taphouse, 5920 Roswell Rd, Sandy Springs, GA
price: Check website for showtimes and tickets
blurb:
Toy Story 5 opens June 19th, and Springs Cinema & Taphouse is literally in your neighborhood. Tom Hanks and Tim Allen are back as Woody and Buzz, and this time the gang is going up against a tablet that thinks she knows better than a box of plastic toys. The fact that every parent in America will immediately recognize that plotline is either reassuring or unsettling, depending on how many screen time battles you have lost this week.

Springs Cinema is the kind of place where you can watch a kids' movie and still have a decent drink in your hand. Multiple showtimes run through the day and into the evening, so you have options whether you are doing a morning matinee or making it a Friday night thing. This one is a MUST if you have young kids, and honestly the adults are going to tear up at least once. You know it, I know it.
scoring_notes: Opening weekend of a major Pixar film is genuinely unique to this specific week. Springs Cinema & Taphouse is a Perimeter-area venue that the audience knows and uses. The mix of young families and the taphouse format serves both the parent crowd and the adult crowd. Strong family-skew event that also has adult appeal through the venue.

event_name: Black Experience Tour at Barrington Hall
venue: Barrington Hall, 535 Barrington Dr, Roswell, GA
blurb:
This is not your standard historic home tour. The Black Experience Tour at Barrington Hall goes straight to the people who actually built the place: the enslaved families who lived and worked there before, during, and after the Civil War. You walk away with a completely different understanding of what early Roswell actually was.

With Juneteenth landing on June 19th, the timing here is not a coincidence and it is not something to skip past. This is the kind of experience that sticks with you. Small group, real history, a guide who has done the work to bring these stories to the surface. If you have older kids or teenagers, bring them. If you don't, go anyway.
scoring_notes: Highly educated audience (70% bachelor's degree or higher) skews toward experiential, educational events. The Juneteenth timing on June 19th makes this especially relevant this week. It is not a recurring mass-market event. The historical weight gives it genuine uniqueness. Strong adult skew, which fits the Perimeter audience well.

venue: Chattahoochee Nature Center, 9135 Willeo Rd, Roswell, GA
blurb:
Father's Day falls on June 21st this year, and if your plan is still "I don't know, maybe brunch," this is a better plan. The Chattahoochee Nature Center is running a guided canoe session on Beaver Pond specifically designed for families, including first-timers and kids who have never been in a canoe before.

The guides walk everyone through technique and gear before you even get on the water, so nobody has to pretend they know what they are doing. It runs 90 minutes, it is outside, and it is the kind of afternoon Dad will actually remember. Spots are limited, which means this is the part where you register instead of waiting until Saturday night.
scoring_notes: Father's Day timing is a major hook. The mix of young families and the median age of 38 makes this nearly perfectly targeted. Guided format lowers the barrier for first-timers. Outdoor, active, and experiential hits the right notes for this educated, active-leaning audience. Limited capacity creates real urgency.

blurb: Alive in Roswell kicks off the week on Canton Street, the kind of evening where you park once and end up staying longer than you planned. Local vendors, live music, and the energy of a street that actually knows how to do this well. Dogs are welcome on leash, which tells you something about the vibe. If you haven't been to Canton Street in a while, or you're looking for a low-key way to start the weekend week, Thursday evening here is an easy yes.
scoring_notes: Alive in Roswell is a recurring event but monthly, not weekly, which gives it enough uniqueness to include. Canton Street is a strong fit for the East Cobb demographic. Opening night of the issue window (June 18) makes it timely. The dog-friendly, walkable format plays well for the mix of families and empty nesters.

event_name: Symbols of the Cemetery Walking Tour
blurb: This one is genuinely different. The Kennesaw Cemetery Preservation Foundation is leading a guided walking tour through the historic Kennesaw City Cemetery, breaking down the symbolism carved into gravestones that most people walk right past. Anchors, hands, lambs, hourglasses. Every symbol tells you something about who was buried there and when. It's local history in the most literal sense, and at 90 minutes on a Sunday evening it's a low-commitment way to learn something you won't find in a classroom. Free to attend, educational without being dry, and the kind of thing you'll bring up at dinner for weeks.
scoring_notes: Strong fit for the educated East Cobb audience (65% bachelor's degree or higher). History and preservation events tend to resonate with established homeowners and the 40+ crowd. Unique format with real educational value. Free makes it an easy yes. Lower audience match than the Braves or wine dinner, but high uniqueness lifts it.

venue: The Art Place at Mountain View, Marietta
blurb: Outdoor movie night at The Art Place on Sandy Plains Road this Saturday, and they're screening the new Superman. Free admission, starts at 7:30, and it's right in the neighborhood. Bring a blanket, drag the teenagers out of the house, grab dinner beforehand. The summer blockbuster under the stars format is hard to beat when the event is this close to home. Just show up.
scoring_notes: Hyper-local to East Cobb, free, family-friendly, and the new Superman is a genuine draw. Teens and families with older kids are a strong match. Lower uniqueness score because outdoor movies recur, but the new Superman title and the proximity to the audience bump it meaningfully.

blurb: Before the 4:10 first pitch Saturday, the Battery is throwing a full-on block party from noon to 4. Live entertainment, nonstop energy, and it's all happening right outside the ballpark. Even if you don't have game tickets, this is worth showing up for. The Battery does this well, and on a summer Saturday with Father's Day weekend in the air, the vibe is going to be dialed up. Pair it with the Brewers game and you've got a proper afternoon out.
scoring_notes: Free pre-game event at the Battery is a strong fit for East Cobb families and empty nesters alike. Easy drive, no cost to attend, natural pairing with the Brewers game makes it a high-value recommendation. Father's Day weekend timing lifts the energy.

blurb: The Chattahoochee Nature Center turns 50 this year, and Wednesday they're throwing a full celebration for it. CNC has been a fixture for this community for half a century, and if you grew up in the area or raised kids here, there's a decent chance you've been there more than once. A 50th birthday event is a one-time milestone, not a regular Saturday program, and it's happening right up the road in Roswell. Worth checking their site for details on what the day looks like and whether you need to grab tickets in advance.
scoring_notes: CNC is deeply familiar to East Cobb families and long-term residents. A 50th anniversary event is genuinely unique and milestone-worthy. The established families and empty nesters in this audience have real nostalgia connections to this institution. Roswell location is convenient.

event_name: Jonata Wine Dinner – 5 Course Tasting Menu
blurb: Jonata Wines comes from the same ownership group as Screaming Eagle, one of the most sought-after labels in California. An intimate five-course tasting dinner built around that kind of pedigree is not something you see on the calendar every week. This is a proper adults-only evening, the kind where the conversation is good, the pours are generous, and you learn something about wine you didn't know before you sat down. Sandy Springs is a short drive from East Cobb and spots at dinners like this tend to go quietly. If a Tuesday night out sounds right, look into it sooner rather than later.
scoring_notes: Screaming Eagle ownership connection gives this event genuine cachet with a $118K median income audience. Intimate format plays well for the empty nester segment. Tuesday night timing reduces the competition from weekend options. High uniqueness score because this type of event is rare on the local calendar.

event_name: Soul, Jazz + Funk Pre-Father's Day Concert
blurb: Russell Thompkins Jr. and The New Stylistics. Skip Martin. The Legendary Blue Notes. Jean Carne. That's a serious lineup for a Saturday night concert the weekend before Father's Day. Mable House Barnes Amphitheatre is an outdoor venue that does these shows right, and this one is built around the kind of soul and funk catalog that actually holds up live. If your dad grew up on this music, or you know someone who did, this is a genuinely good night out. Not a tribute act situation. These are the real acts. Get your tickets early.
scoring_notes: Median age of 42 in East Cobb means many readers have parents in the target demo for this music, plus empty nesters in the audience who know these artists well. Pre-Father's Day framing is timely. Live soul and funk at an outdoor amphitheater is a strong match for this income bracket.

blurb: Three-game home series this weekend against the Brewers, and Sunday's game comes with a free Braves LED Light giveaway for kids. If you've got teenagers who grew up going to games, or you're looking for a Father's Day weekend excuse to get the family to the ballpark, this is it. Truist Park is basically in your backyard, the Battery has options for before and after, and a Sunday afternoon first pitch at 1:35 means you're home in time for dinner. Grab tickets before the good seats are gone.
scoring_notes: Truist Park is a 10-minute drive for most of East Cobb. Father's Day weekend timing is perfect for this audience. Families with teens, established households, $118K median income means ticket prices are not a barrier. Three-game series gives multiple options. Sunday giveaway adds a reason to pick that specific game.

event_name: Music on the Hill: The M-80s – Explosive 80s Rock
blurb:
If you were anywhere near a radio in the 1980s, Friday night in Roswell is calling your name.

The M-80s bring their explosive 80s rock set to the lawn at Roswell City Hall for Music on the Hill, the monthly outdoor concert series that turns a Friday evening into something worth leaving the house for. It's free, it's outside, and it's the kind of music that makes people forget they have early morning plans.

Bring a blanket or some chairs, grab dinner nearby first, and settle in. First note drops at 7:00 PM. Two hours of 80s rock under the summer sky, no ticket required.
scoring_notes: Median age of 42 means this audience was literally growing up during the 80s. Free outdoor concert on a Friday evening is a near-perfect fit for both the established families and empty nester segments. Monthly series but each act is different, so this specific night is its own event worth flagging.

event_name: Historic Roswell Photography Competition and Exhibit
blurb:
The Roswell Photographic Society and the Roswell Historical Society are hosting a photography competition and exhibit starting June 17, timed to the country's 250th anniversary.

It's a look at historic Roswell through local lenses, literally. The kind of quiet, worthwhile exhibit that's easy to miss and worth going out of your way for, especially if history or photography is your thing.

Check visitroswellga.com for venue details and hours.
scoring_notes: The America 250 tie-in adds relevance and a sense of occasion. Well-suited to the educated, culturally engaged portion of this audience. Photography and local history are solid niche interests that map well to the demographic. Slight penalty for geographic stretch (Roswell) and missing venue/time specifics. More of a niche pick than a broad crowd-pleaser.

blurb:
The African Americans for the Arts (AAFTA) is hosting a free Juneteenth Artists Market at the Roswell Cultural Arts Center on June 19.

Local artists, a gallery setting, and a community gathering that actually means something. It's the kind of event that's easy to walk through on your lunch break or after work, and easy to bring someone along who'll appreciate it.

Free to attend. Just show up and look around.
scoring_notes: Free community arts event timed to a culturally significant holiday. The highly educated Perimeter audience tends to engage well with arts events. Slight geographic stretch (Roswell). Missing exact timing in the data, which limits how confidently it can be featured. Lower uniqueness than the City Springs Juneteenth event, which is why it ranks below candidate 3.

event_name: Summer Sippin' Block Party at Southern Post
time: TBD (during Alive in Roswell, 5:00 PM – 9:00 PM)
blurb:
Southern Post is hosting a Summer Sippin' Block Party on June 18, timed alongside Alive in Roswell. Think sip, stroll, and settle in at one of Roswell's best new spots.

It's an easy Thursday night: walk the festival, duck into the block party, try something cold. The combination of the outdoor festival energy and a dedicated drinking event makes it more than the sum of its parts.

Catch it from 5–9 PM. Check Southern Post for specifics on what's being poured.
scoring_notes: Southern Post is a well-regarded destination in the broader Perimeter area. The block party angle makes it feel more special than a regular Alive in Roswell stop. Adult-skewing, which fits. Slight geographic stretch since Roswell is outside the core Perimeter zone, and the candidate data is missing specific timing and pricing details.

event_name: Dueling Pianos at Morty's Meat and Supply
venue: Morty's Meat and Supply, 5509 Chamblee Dunwoody Rd., Dunwoody
blurb:
Dueling Pianos is back on the Smokehouse Stage at Morty's this Friday and Saturday night.

If you've never been to one of these, the format is simple: two pianists, a catalog of every song you know, and a crowd that's basically the show. Requests, sing-alongs, and enough energy to make it feel more like a concert than dinner out. Morty's is already a good time on its own, and this turns it up a level.

Check Morty's for showtime and any cover details. Worth it either night.
scoring_notes: Dueling Pianos runs every Friday and Saturday, which hurts the uniqueness score. But it's a strong match for the adult-skewing Perimeter audience looking for a fun night out, and Morty's is a local anchor. The casual, high-energy format fits the young professional and empty nester segments well. Lower uniqueness because it's a weekly recurring event, but it's the kind of thing readers may not know about.

event_name: Blair Crimmins and the Hookers at MJCCA Courtyard Concert
blurb:
If you haven't seen Blair Crimmins and the Hookers live, this is your moment.

They're one of the Southeast's most fun bands: hot jazz, vintage swing, big brass, and a crowd that can't sit still. The MJCCA Courtyard is an ideal setting for it. Outdoor, local, and the kind of show that turns a Monday night into a story you tell later.

Check the MJCCA site for tickets and start time. These courtyard concerts fill up.
scoring_notes: Live music at a neighborhood venue with a well-regarded regional act. The vintage jazz-swing sound is a strong fit for an educated, mixed-age audience. Courtyard setting keeps it casual and accessible. The MJCCA is a known anchor in this community, which helps familiarity. Deducting slightly for missing start time and price in the candidate data.

event_name: World Cup USA vs. Australia Watch Party on The Lawn
blurb:
The US is in the World Cup, and there's a free watch party happening right in your backyard.

Ashford Lane is setting up a big screen on The Lawn for USA vs. Australia on June 19, 2–5 PM. Drinks to-go available, so grab one and find a spot. This is one of those watch parties where the energy in the crowd makes it better than watching at home, guaranteed.

No tickets, no registration, just show up. Bring the kids, bring a friend, and root loud.
scoring_notes: World Cup only comes around every four years. This is a time-sensitive, genuinely rare event happening literally on the Perimeter at a venue this audience already knows. Free entry removes all friction. Appeals to young professionals, families, and empty nesters equally. The adult social atmosphere (drinks, big crowd energy) skews well for this audience.

event_name: Lunch and Learn: Safe at Home
blurb:
Thrive in Lewisville is hosting a "Safe at Home" Lunch and Learn on Tuesday, June 9. The session runs an hour and focuses on home safety, which is a topic that hits differently when you're a homeowner with kids in the house.

This one's more niche than a festival or a concert, but for the right reader, it's worth knowing about. If you've been putting off a conversation about home safety or want practical information without a sales pitch, this is a low-commitment way to get it.

Free, an hour long, and right in Lewisville. RSVP if you plan to attend.
scoring_notes: 65% homeownership rate and strong family skew make home safety content relevant. However, the 4pm time listed as 'Lunch and Learn' is an inconsistency that suggests possible data error. Lower scores reflect narrower appeal and lower uniqueness. Included as a utility pick for the right reader segment.

blurb: Barrington Hall in Historic Roswell is one of the most beautiful spots in the area, and the Roswell Lavender Festival turns it into something even better for one day. You get a juried fine arts and handmade crafts market set on the shaded historic grounds, surrounded by gardens in peak early-summer form. It is the kind of outing that is easy to make a morning or afternoon of, especially if you combine it with lunch on Canton Street nearby. A short drive from the Perimeter and a genuinely pretty way to spend a Saturday.
scoring_notes: The educated, higher-income Perimeter audience appreciates fine arts markets and historic settings. The Barrington Hall venue adds real appeal. Loses points on uniqueness because arts festivals recur seasonally, and lavender festivals specifically feel like a familiar format. Still a solid inclusion for variety and for the audience segments (empty nesters, adult couples) who genuinely enjoy this type of outing.

blurb: Aurora Cineplex is running a Big Screen Classics series this year, and two of the 12 screenings fall in your week. They are showing on the big screen in luxury heated recliners, which is a genuinely better way to watch an older film than your couch. The specific titles showing this week were not available at press time, so check Aurora's site for the lineup. If you have been meaning to see a classic the way it was meant to be seen, this is a low-effort, high-reward way to spend a Sunday or a Wednesday night.
scoring_notes: The educated, adult-skewing Perimeter audience is a reasonable fit for a classics film series. The luxury recliner format and big-screen classic combination is a step above a typical movie night, which helps. Loses points on uniqueness because it is a recurring monthly series and we do not know the specific film showing this week. Still a solid lower-tier pick for variety in the lineup.

blurb: Roswell Empowered is throwing a Pride Mini Golf Night at The Fringe on Wednesday, and everyone is welcome. It is a casual, fun mid-week event to celebrate Pride Week in Roswell with mini golf, good company, and a welcoming crowd. The Fringe is a newer addition to the Roswell scene and worth checking out if you have not been. Low stakes, high fun, and a nice way to cap the week. Check Roswell Empowered's page for ticket details and start time.
scoring_notes: The Perimeter audience skews younger and more urban-adjacent than East Cobb, which makes this event a reasonable fit. Pride events during Pride Week have natural urgency. Mini golf keeps it accessible and casual rather than niche. Slightly outside the Perimeter core (Roswell), but close enough. Loses a point on demographic fit because the audience is mixed and the event is more niche than the top picks.

time: Sat 9:00 AM to 3:00 PM, Sun 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM
blurb: The 27th Annual Flying Colors Butterfly Festival is back at Chattahoochee Nature Center, and it is one of those events that genuinely delivers for everyone in the car. Walk through a live butterfly encounter, meet pollinator experts, and take part in the Migration Game spread across the whole festival. It runs both Saturday and Sunday, so there is plenty of time to fit it in. Families with younger kids will love it. Adults who appreciate the outdoors and a little learning will too. It is a short drive from the Perimeter and one of the better warm-weather traditions in the area.
scoring_notes: A recurring annual festival, which keeps uniqueness moderate. But 27 years in means it is an established, reliable community event rather than a throwaway. The Perimeter has a mix of young families and adults, and this event works for both. Chattahoochee Nature Center is a beloved local institution. Scores well enough to include, especially given the weekend-long run and the live butterfly encounter as a genuine draw.

blurb: If you have walked past Spruill Gallery a hundred times and never stopped in, this Saturday is the one to finally do it. The gallery is hosting a live meet-the-artist event for their current exhibition, "Returns," where you can actually talk to the people who made the work on the walls. It is the kind of thing that turns a passive gallery visit into a real conversation. The Perimeter has a serious arts scene, and Spruill is the heart of it. Free to attend, no pretense required. Worth an hour of your Saturday.
scoring_notes: High education level (70% with a bachelor's or higher) and solid household income suggest this audience engages with arts and culture. The artist engagement format is meaningfully different from just seeing an exhibit, which bumps uniqueness. Spruill Gallery is directly in the coverage area, which is a plus. Scores a bit lower on audience match because it is a niche draw compared to the block party or World Cup event.

blurb: Sunday plans just got better. Chef Michelle Pellegri of Roswell Baking Company is teaching a hands-on Neapolitan pizza making class, and it is the kind of afternoon that is genuinely fun whether you cook a lot or not at all. You will learn the technique, make the dough, build the pizza, and eat what you made. It is a short drive from the Perimeter and a great pick for a date night, a friend group outing, or just something different to do on a Sunday. Spots are limited, so if this sounds like your kind of thing, do not wait.
scoring_notes: The Perimeter audience skews educated, well-paid, and experience-oriented. Hands-on cooking classes are a strong fit for young professionals and couples without kids, and the Neapolitan style gives it a slightly elevated, foodie-adjacent angle. Not a Perimeter-area venue (it is in Roswell), but close enough and specific enough to be worth including. Loses a point on uniqueness because cooking classes recur, but this is a specific instructor and style.

blurb: Friday night in Dunwoody is about to get loud. The Funwoody Restaurant Courtyard is hosting a full-on block party with DJ Fernando spinning a live video music mix of your favorite 80s, 90s, and disco hits. Think outdoor bar energy, great food from the surrounding restaurants, and a crowd that actually knows every word to every song. It is sponsored by the DHS Class of '86, which tells you exactly what kind of night this will be. Free to attend, walkable if you live nearby, and the perfect way to kick off a weekend. Go find your dancing shoes.
scoring_notes: Median age of 38 means this audience grew up with 80s and 90s music. The Perimeter skews more adult than family, so a Friday night outdoor dance party is a strong fit. Funwoody is a well-known local gathering spot. The nostalgia angle and outdoor block party format give it good energy. Loses a point on uniqueness because DJ block parties do recur, but the specific format and sponsor make this one feel like an event rather than a routine night out.

event_name: World Cup Kickoff Party on The Lawn
blurb: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is finally here, and it is literally being played in your backyard. Atlanta is a host city, and this Saturday Ashford Lane is throwing a full-on celebration to kick things off on The Lawn. Kids activities, soccer experiences, food, and the energy of a city that knows it has a front-row seat to the biggest sporting event on the planet. This is not just a typical watch party. It is the start of something that only happens once every four years, and this time the world is coming to us. Free to attend, right in the neighborhood, and the kind of afternoon you will want to be part of. Get out there.
scoring_notes: The 2026 World Cup is a once-in-a-generation event hosted in Atlanta. This kickoff party is hyper-local (Ashford Lane is literally in the Perimeter coverage area), free, and appeals to the full audience mix: young professionals, young families, and sports fans of all ages. Uniqueness score is a 10 because the World Cup coming to Atlanta is a historic moment and this is the official community celebration of it.

blurb:
If you want a Saturday night that actually feels like a Saturday night, Grupo Rye is playing live at Block and Drum in Chamblee. It's a full live music experience built to get people on the floor, running until midnight.

Chamblee's music scene is underrated and Block and Drum is a solid room. This one is for the crowd that wants energy over ambiance.

Doors are at 8. Show up ready to move.
scoring_notes: Live music is broadly appealing but Grupo Rye is a Spanish-language band, which may not map as cleanly to the core Perimeter demographic. Chamblee is slightly outside the coverage area. Uniqueness is decent since it's a live touring act, not a DJ night. Lower audience match score reflects the demographic gap.

event_name: Saturday Night at Politan Row with DJ Mathis
blurb:
Politan Row turns up Saturday night with DJ Mathis on the decks. If you haven't been lately, it's the right kind of spot for this: food hall energy, good drinks, and a vibe that doesn't require you to dress up or make a reservation.

Bring your crew, grab something from whichever vendor sounds good, and just hang. It's a Saturday night that comes together without any real planning.

Shows up regularly, so think of this as a reminder to actually go this week instead of putting it off again.
scoring_notes: Politan Row is a well-known Perimeter-area destination and DJ nights have broad adult appeal. Lower uniqueness because this appears to be a recurring Saturday event (dates show 5/30 and 6/6), not a one-time thing. Still a valid recommendation for a lower-stakes pick. Good fit for young professionals and social empty nesters.

event_name: Passport to Flavor Kids Cooking Camp
venue: Mise en Place Cooking Studio, Roswell
blurb:
Summer camp season is here, and this one is worth knowing about if you've got kids ages 6 to 8. Mise en Place in Roswell is running a three-day international cooking camp where kids make real food: handmade pizza dough, guacamole from scratch, chocolate mousse, crepes. They eat everything they cook, and they get a little passport to track their culinary travels.

It runs Monday through Wednesday, June 1 to 3, so it's a great way to fill the first week of summer with something genuinely fun and skill-building.

Spots fill up fast for cooking camps like this. If it sounds right for your kid, don't wait.
scoring_notes: Good fit for the young families segment of the Perimeter audience. Roswell is slightly outside core coverage but accessible. Higher income audience can absorb camp pricing without hesitation. Lower audience match because the newsletter skews more adult overall; this is a niche pick for the parent subset. Uniqueness is solid: themed multi-day cooking camp is not an everyday offering.

event_name: Global Running Day: Run, Walk & Social at Brook Run Park
blurb:
Global Running Day is June 4th, and Dunwoody is celebrating it a day early with Big Peach Running Co. at Brook Run Park. It's a run, a walk, and a social after, all paces and abilities welcome.

Brook Run is a great park and this is a genuinely easy yes: it's free, it's in the neighborhood, it's midweek, and you end with people. The social part is the whole reason to go.

Show up, move your body, meet someone new. Wednesday nights could use more of this.
scoring_notes: Active, health-conscious crowd at 38 median age is a natural fit for a running event. Dunwoody is in the coverage area. Free and low-friction. The Global Running Day tie-in gives it a reason to exist beyond a regular group run. Lower uniqueness score because group runs happen regularly, but the official Global Running Day hook and city partnership elevate it slightly.

event_name: Creative Caviar: A Guided Caviar Tasting
blurb:
You don't need to know anything about caviar to enjoy this. That's basically the whole point. Creative Caviar is a guided tasting designed to be approachable, not stuffy. Think curiosity over formality.

It's happening at the Distillery of Modern Art in Chamblee, which is already a cool room before you add caviar to the mix. Sunday afternoon, a couple hours, something genuinely different to do with your weekend.

If you've always been a little curious but never knew where to start, this is your in. Spots are limited, so don't sleep on it.
scoring_notes: High uniqueness: guided caviar tasting is not a common Atlanta event format. Strong fit for the educated, higher-income Perimeter audience that gravitates toward food and drink experiences. Chamblee is slightly outside core coverage area but close enough. Adult-only skew is a plus for this newsletter's more adult-leaning audience.

event_name: Zane Lamprey Live at Pontoon Brewing
blurb:
If you know Zane Lamprey, you already know this is going to be a good time. If you don't, here's the short version: he's an Amazon Prime comedian who built his career traveling the world drinking with strangers, and somehow turned that into a real career. The man has range.

He's doing one night at Pontoon Brewing in Sandy Springs, which means a craft beer in your hand, a comedian on stage, and nowhere you need to be Monday morning. That's a Sunday well spent.

Tickets won't last long for a room this size. Grab them now and figure out who's coming with you later.
scoring_notes: Adult-skewing, local venue with a built-in social atmosphere. Sandy Springs address is directly in the coverage area. Comedy at a brewery is a strong fit for the 38-median-age, young-professional and empty-nester crowd. One-night-only touring act adds real urgency. Family audiences can sit this one out.

blurb:
Twenty-five of Sandy Springs' most talked-about restaurants, all in one place, one night only. Food That Rocks takes over City Green with bites, cocktails, and the kind of energy that makes a Thursday feel like a Friday.

This is the fastest way to find your new favorite spot without committing to a full dinner at a place you've never tried. Graze, sip, compare notes, repeat. It's built for exactly the kind of night out that's easy to talk yourself out of but impossible to regret.

It lands right at the end of the week, so bring someone worth eating with. Browse the lineup ahead of time, come hungry, and make a reservation somewhere nearby for after.
scoring_notes: One-night-only event in Sandy Springs, squarely in the Perimeter coverage area. 25-restaurant format is high-novelty and adult-skewing, perfect for young professionals and date-night empty nesters. $105K median income means ticket price (likely $50-80 range for this format) is a non-issue. Strong uniqueness: annual or semi-annual at best, not a recurring weekly thing.
